criterion: A user can sign up and log in with email and password.
note: Auth screen rendered with email/password fields and a Sign up/Sign in toggle. Unaffected by this cycle's change_log (FR-001/003/004/009 only).

criterion: All elements previously styled with the purple theme color now render using #A0B9BF, applied uniformly across buttons, message bubbles, badges, links/focus rings, and auth screen accents.
note: Smoke check: auth screen primary button still renders in the #A0B9BF accent; theme.css untouched by this cycle. dist CSS scan confirmed the only accent hex present is #a0b9bf.

criterion: A user can sign up and log in with email and password.
note: Signed up a fresh test account and landed in the authenticated chat view (project has auto-confirm enabled).

criterion: A single chat box lets the user type and send a message.
note: One composer (textarea + Send button) visible, no multi-panel UI. Unaffected by this cycle.

criterion: The chat UI displays partial response text as it arrives, before the full completion is received.
note: Captured mid-stream bubble text: "Nice, Inception is a " (not yet the full final reply), proving progressive rendering rather than full-response buffering. Unaffected by this cycle.

criterion: After a successful Supabase insert, the UI shows a confirmation that the row was written.
note: 'Saved · Inception' success badge (green) rendered under the assistant turn after the <ADD> tag was parsed and the items row inserted.

criterion: The interface is a clean, minimal chat-bubble style with no custom branding.
note: Chat panel shows plain rounded message bubbles, a plain text header, no logo/wordmark.

criterion: The parser correctly extracts an <ADD> tag whether it appears at the beginning, middle, or end of the streamed response.
note: Browser-verified for the end position here; start/middle positions are covered by src/lib/__tests__/tagParser.test.ts (unchanged this cycle, still passing — 19/19 tagParser tests green).

criterion: On successful <ADD> extraction, a row is inserted into the Supabase items table with the parsed item and rating.
note: Live REST query (GET /rest/v1/items) returned: [{"item":"Inception","rating":5,"created_at":"2026-07-14T23:36:37.564695+00:00"}]. Row with item='Inception', rating=5 found.

criterion: The parser is structured to register/handle multiple tag types (extensible), demonstrated in code.
note: src/lib/tagParser.ts TagRegistry/TagDefinition pattern; createDefaultTagRegistry() now registers TWO definitions (ADD_TAG_DEFINITION, UPDATE_TAG_DEFINITION), proving the registry genuinely supports more than one tag type end to end, not just architecturally.

criterion: The parser functionally extracts a THIRD tag type <UPDATE> as a registered tag, in addition to <ADD> and <RECOMMEND>.
note: Scripted mock reply containing <UPDATE item="Inception" rating="2" /> was parsed by the real tagParser.ts/useChat.ts code path and dispatched to the UPDATE handler, distinct from <ADD>.

criterion: On successful <UPDATE> extraction, a NEW row is inserted into the items table (rating history preserved) — it does NOT overwrite the existing row.
note: See FR-009 Supabase-row evidence below: two 'Inception' rows exist after this turn (rating 5 then rating 2), proving insert-with-history, not an overwrite/upsert.

criterion: <ADD> and <RECOMMEND> are dispatched to different handlers: <ADD> writes a row; <RECOMMEND> is display-only with no database write.
note: <RECOMMEND> (FR-008) remains unimplemented in this codebase (createDefaultTagRegistry() registers only ADD/UPDATE — confirmed by code review of src/lib/tagParser.ts). This is a pre-existing gap first flagged in the Cycle-3 (v4) QA pass, not introduced or regressed by this cycle, and out of this cycle's explicit change_log scope (FR-001/003/004/009 only). <ADD> vs <UPDATE> dispatch (both registered, different handlers/badges) IS fully verified above.

criterion: When the user re-mentions an already-logged title with an opinion, the assistant emits an inline <UPDATE item="..." rating="..." /> tag rather than a new <ADD>.
note: Re-mention turn produced a distinct 'Rating updated · Inception' badge (not 'Saved · Inception'), proving the client dispatched an <UPDATE> match, not an <ADD> match.

criterion: Same-title matching is performed model-side: the model is given the calling user's own existing logged titles (read server-side, RLS-respecting) and judges fuzzily (accounting for typos/case/phrasing) whether the new opinion refers to an already-logged title.
note: Model-side fuzzy judgment requires a live OpenAI call; no OPENAI_API_KEY is available in this sandbox (this QA harness's mock server stands in for the edge function's HTTP contract only, not the model's reasoning). Verified instead by: (1) code review of netlify/edge-functions/chat.ts's fetchExistingTitlesMessage(), which builds a fresh per-request Supabase client authenticated with the caller's own access token (never service-role) and reads only `items` rows visible under that token; (2) src/lib/__tests__/systemPrompt.test.ts's buildExistingTitlesMessage() tests (dedup, null-when-empty, correct instruction text referencing typos/case/phrasing); (3) this QA pass's own live-Supabase RLS check (below) confirming that exact read pattern (select scoped to auth.uid()) is enforced server-side, not just trusted client-side.

criterion: If the re-mentioned title does not match any prior log for that user, the assistant falls through to a normal <ADD> instead of <UPDATE>.
note: Demonstrated by the first turn of this test run: a title with no prior log ('Inception', first mention) produced <ADD>, not <UPDATE> — the same registry/dispatch code path used for re-mentions, just with no match. Model-side 'no match found' branch is prompt-documented (systemPrompt.ts: 'If no reference list has been provided, or the title isn't in it, treat the movie as new and use <ADD>') and unit-tested indirectly via buildExistingTitlesMessage returning null for a user with no items.

criterion: On successful <UPDATE>, a NEW row is inserted into the items table (item, rating, category, raw_user_text, created_at) — the existing row is NOT overwritten, and full rating history for the title is preserved.
note:
Live REST query (GET /rest/v1/items?item=eq.Inception) returned: [{"item":"Inception","rating":5,"created_at":"2026-07-14T23:36:37.564695+00:00"}, 
 {"item":"Inception","rating":2,"created_at":"2026-07-14T23:36:38.686749+00:00"}]. Expected exactly 2 historical rows (rating 5, then rating 2) — confirmed.

criterion: No new table or column is added for updates; <UPDATE> reuses the existing items table (additive-only, no migration).
note: supabase/migrations/002_items.sql (schema) was not modified this cycle; the only new migration (005_parse_failures_unrecognized_title_reason.sql) widens a CHECK constraint on parse_failures.reason, unrelated to the items table shape. Confirmed by code/migration-directory review.

criterion: The <UPDATE> read of the user's existing titles and the <UPDATE> insert are both scoped to the user's own user_id via existing RLS — no cross-user data is read or written.
note: The items insert above went through supabase-js as the authenticated user (RLS items_insert_own policy: auth.uid() = user_id). Independently, this QA pass's live-project script signed up two throwaway users and confirmed: cross-user SELECT on items returns [], and an INSERT forging another user's user_id is rejected with Postgres 42501 (row-level security policy violation) — the exact isolation boundary FR-009's read/insert path relies on.

criterion: An <UPDATE> renders in the UI as a distinct 'rating updated' confirmation, visually distinguishable from the <ADD> 'logged' confirmation.
note: Screenshot shows a slate-blue-accent 'Rating updated · Inception' badge (Badge tone='update', src/components/ui/Badge.css .ui-badge--update: --color-accent-soft bg / --color-accent-text text), visually distinct from the earlier green 'Saved · Inception' badge (tone='success').

criterion: A malformed or missing <UPDATE> tag (when an update was expected) produces a fallback message, logs the raw output to parse_failures, and is subject to the same 2-retry silent-discard behavior as <ADD>.
note: Scripted reply with <UPDATE item="Inception" /> (missing required 'rating' attribute) produced the same 'Couldn't log that — logged for review.' danger badge as a malformed <ADD> (src/lib/tagParser.ts validates ADD/UPDATE with the identical validateItemRatingAttrs, confirmed by src/lib/__tests__/tagParser.test.ts's dedicated 'flags a malformed <UPDATE> the same way as a malformed <ADD>' test). Note: this scenario is a recognized-malformed tag, which useChat.ts intentionally does NOT retry (retry is reserved for a clear opinion producing NO tag at all, not a broken one) — that distinction is exercised separately below.

criterion: A malformed or missing <UPDATE> tag (when an update was expected) produces a fallback message and the raw output is logged, following the same discipline and 2-retry silent-discard behavior as <ADD>.
note: Same evidence as FR-009 above — malformed <UPDATE> handling is identical code path to malformed <ADD>.

criterion: The raw model output is logged (retrievable for debugging) whenever tag extraction fails.
note: Live REST query (GET /rest/v1/parse_failures) returned: [{"raw_output":"Updating that now. <UPDATE item=\"Inception\" />","reason":"malformed"}]. A reason='malformed' row containing the raw <UPDATE ...> output was found.

criterion: When the model does not recognize a stated title as a real, existing movie, it asks for clarification/confirmation in its reply instead of emitting an <ADD>/<UPDATE> tag.
note: Scripted reply matching the system prompt's required 'don't recognize' + 'movie' phrasing rendered as an ordinary conversational bubble with NO write-confirmation badge (no <ADD>/<UPDATE> was parsed) and no items row was written for 'Freeze Frame 3000' (see Supabase check below).

criterion: When the model does not recognize a stated title as a real movie, it asks for clarification instead of emitting a tag, and the raw output is logged to parse_failures with reason 'unrecognized_title'.
note: Confirmed via live Supabase REST check below (parse_failures row with reason='unrecognized_title' and the clarification text present).

criterion: A strongly positive phrasing ('loved') produces a higher rating value than a lukewarm phrasing ('liked'), which produces a higher value than a negative phrasing ('hated').
note: This is a live-model rating-inference behavior (systemPrompt.ts's 1-5 intensity ladder, unchanged this cycle except for the temperature/unrecognized-title additions) — requires a real, non-deterministic OpenAI call to observe comparative outputs across 3 separate prompts, which this sandbox cannot make (no OPENAI_API_KEY). Unchanged behavior per this cycle's own work order ('Existing happy-path <ADD> and <RECOMMEND> emission behavior unchanged'); the rating-scale instructions themselves are asserted present by src/lib/__tests__/systemPrompt.test.ts.

criterion: When the opinion-heuristic fires and no tag is returned, the system retries the OpenAI call silently up to 2 additional times before falling back; only the final attempt's output (success or fallback) is streamed to the user.
note: See FR-004 retry-succeeds/retry-fails evidence below — this criterion is verified there with call-count proof; noted here for completeness since the unrecognized-title turn above deliberately did NOT retry (see next criterion).

criterion: The system prompt is defined in the codebase and is reviewable in the repo.
note: src/lib/systemPrompt.ts exports SYSTEM_PROMPT (now including the Cycle-4 <UPDATE>-emission and unrecognized-title-clarification clauses), imported by netlify/edge-functions/chat.ts and unit-tested in src/lib/__tests__/systemPrompt.test.ts (19 assertions, all passing).

criterion: unrecognized_title clarification does not trigger the silent retry loop
note:
Mock server call counter for the 'Freeze Frame 3000' trigger = 1 (expected exactly 1 — no retry attempted, since useChat.ts's looksLikeUnrecognizedTitleClarification() branch is checked before the retryable/no-tag branch). Live REST parse_failures query returned: [{"raw_output":"Hmm, I don't recognize that as a real movie — could you double-check the title?","reason":"unrecognized_title"}, 
 {"raw_output":"Updating that now. <UPDATE item=\"Inception\" />","reason":"malformed"}].

criterion: A clearly negative or neutral opinion on a real movie (e.g. 'I hated Barbie', 'Marty Supreme was fine') reliably produces a tag (<ADD> or <UPDATE>) rather than intermittently producing none.
note: Simulated the exact defect this cycle fixes: the mocked model missed the tag on attempts 1-2 and only complied on attempt 3. Mock server call counter confirms exactly 3 attempts were made (1 original + 2 retries). The user only ever saw the FINAL attempt's text ("Got it!") — the 2 discarded "Hmm, tell me more..." replies never appeared in the UI. No parse_failures row was logged for this turn (a compliance-miss-then-recovery is not a failure). Combined with the temperature reduction to 0.2 (src/lib/openaiRequest.ts, documented rationale in-file), this is the two-layer fix for FR-001 Issue 1.

criterion: When the opinion-heuristic fires and no tag is returned, the system retries the OpenAI call silently up to 2 additional times before falling back; only the final attempt's output (success or fallback) is streamed to the user.
note: Mock server call counter for the 'RetryProbeAlpha' trigger = 3 (expected 3: 1 original + 2 silent retries before the loop found a tag on the 3rd). Final rendered bubble text was the 3rd attempt's output only ("Got it!"), never the 1st/2nd attempts' discarded text — matching useChat.ts's runAttempt()/retry-loop implementation (also covered by a dedicated unit test in useChat.test.ts).

criterion: No tag types beyond <ADD>, <RECOMMEND>, and <UPDATE> are shipped in this build.
note: createDefaultTagRegistry() (src/lib/tagParser.ts) registers exactly ADD_TAG_DEFINITION and UPDATE_TAG_DEFINITION — 2 tag types total (RECOMMEND remains unbuilt, not a 3rd shipped type). Confirmed by code review.

criterion: On successful <ADD> extraction, a row is inserted into the Supabase items table with the parsed item and rating.
note: Live REST query (GET /rest/v1/items?item=eq.RetryProbeAlpha) returned: [{"item":"RetryProbeAlpha","rating":5}] — confirms the item was written only once, from the successful 3rd attempt, not duplicated per retry.

criterion: After 3 failed attempts (1 original + 2 retries) with no tag, a fallback message is shown and the raw output is logged to parse_failures with reason 'missing'.
note: Mock server call counter for the 'RetryProbeBeta' trigger = 3 (expected exactly 3 — the model never complies, so all 3 attempts run and none are retried further). Exactly 1 parse_failures row(s) with reason='missing' found (expected exactly 1 — logged once after exhausting attempts, not once per attempt). UI showed the neutral 'Didn't catch an item to log there.' footnote.

criterion: When the model emits a malformed tag, the user sees a fallback message rather than a silent failure.
note: Non-self-closing <ADD ...> tag from the mocked model produced a 'Couldn't log that — logged for review.' danger badge instead of a silent/crashed UI. Unaffected by this cycle.

criterion: No unhandled exception surfaces to the end user during adversarial/pressure testing.
note: Playwright page.on('pageerror') listener attached for the entire run (covering every scenario above, including malformed/retry/unrecognized-title turns); 0 page error(s) recorded.

criterion: An off-topic or ambiguous message ('the movie was okay') is handled gracefully with a sensible conversational reply and no crash.
note: Ambiguous input (no specific title) produced a clarifying conversational reply, no tag, no badge, no crash. Unaffected by this cycle.

criterion: When the model omits a tag, the user sees a clear fallback message rather than nothing or an error crash.
note: Off-topic/prompt-injection style message produced a normal steered-back conversational reply (no tag omission treated as an error since no loggable opinion was expressed). Unaffected by this cycle.

criterion: A malformed or missing <RECOMMEND> tag (when a recommendation was expected) produces a fallback message and the raw output is logged, not a silent failure or crash.
note: <RECOMMEND> (FR-008) is not implemented in this build — pre-existing gap, not this cycle's scope (change_log v5 touches FR-001/003/004/009 only). Not applicable this cycle.

criterion: When the user asks for a recommendation but has no logged items yet, the bot responds gracefully (sensible conversational reply, no crash, no fabricated personalized recommendation).
note: <RECOMMEND>/FR-008 is not implemented in this build — pre-existing gap, not applicable this cycle.

criterion: The function calls the OpenAI API and does not use a hardcoded/mock response.
note: Verified by code review only: netlify/edge-functions/chat.ts performs a real fetch() to https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions using Deno.env.get('OPENAI_API_KEY') and buildOpenAIRequestBody({..., temperature: OPENAI_TEMPERATURE}). No OpenAI key is available in this QA sandbox, so this pass uses a local scripted server standing in for the edge function's HTTP contract to exercise the frontend streaming/parsing/persistence pipeline end-to-end; unchanged by this cycle except the temperature value.

criterion: Sending 'I loved Inception' returns a conversational reply that also contains an inline tagged block of the form <ADD item="Inception" rating="<value>" />.
note: Reproduced with a scripted mock reply matching the exact contract the real system prompt specifies; end-to-end parsing/display/Supabase-write verified above.

criterion: When the user explicitly asks for a recommendation, the model emits an inline <RECOMMEND item="..." reason="..." /> tag alongside its conversational reply.
note: <RECOMMEND> (FR-008) is not implemented in this build — pre-existing gap predating this cycle, not in this cycle's change_log scope.

criterion: The model does NOT emit a <RECOMMEND> tag when the user has not explicitly asked for a recommendation (no proactive insertion into unrelated replies).
note: Vacuously true: no <RECOMMEND> tag definition is registered anywhere in this build, so it is structurally impossible for one to be emitted/parsed proactively or otherwise. Not a meaningful behavioral test of intent-gating (that would require FR-008 to actually exist), but confirms no regression toward accidentally parsing an unregistered tag.

criterion: The recommendation is grounded in the calling user's own logged items provided to the model at request time.
note: <RECOMMEND> (FR-008) is not implemented in this build — pre-existing gap, not applicable this cycle.

criterion: The OpenAI call is made at a reduced, conservative temperature, and the exact chosen value is documented in the repo (system prompt file or edge function comment).
note: src/lib/openaiRequest.ts exports OPENAI_TEMPERATURE = 0.2 with an in-file comment documenting the rationale (dev-chosen conservative value, low end of the interviewer's illustrative 0.2-0.3 range) and buildOpenAIRequestBody() includes it in the request sent to OpenAI; netlify/edge-functions/chat.ts calls buildOpenAIRequestBody(messages) directly. Asserted by src/lib/__tests__/openaiRequest.test.ts (2/2 passing).

criterion: There is no admin/reviewer role and no cross-account aggregate view in this build.
note: Auth screen only offers sign-in/sign-up for the single 'user' role; no role selector or admin entry point exists anywhere in the UI. Unaffected by this cycle.

criterion: A logged-in user's chat history and logged items persist and are visible after logging out and back in.
note: After signing out and back in as the same user, the full prior conversation from this session reloaded from chat_messages, including turns from this cycle's new <UPDATE>/retry/unrecognized-title scenarios. Unaffected by this cycle's changes.

criterion: A user cannot see another user's chat history or logged items.
note: Brand-new second user sees the empty-state prompt in the UI (not user A's conversation, including this cycle's new <UPDATE>/retry rows). Live REST cross-check as user B: GET /rest/v1/items -> [], GET /rest/v1/chat_messages -> [] (both empty). Independently re-verified this cycle with a dedicated throwaway two-user script directly against the same live project: cross-user SELECTs on items/chat_messages returned [] and a forged cross-user INSERT was rejected with Postgres 42501 (row-level security policy violation) — RLS unaffected by the FR-009 <UPDATE> insert path, which reuses the same items_insert_own policy.

criterion: The tag is extracted correctly even though the response is consumed incrementally as a stream.
note: <ADD>/<UPDATE> tags arrived split across multiple SSE chunks from the mock streaming server and were still correctly parsed and written throughout this run (see FR-003/FR-009 Supabase-row evidence).

criterion: There is no full-response buffering that blocks display until completion.
note: Mid-stream screenshot FR-002-1 shows partial bubble text before the final reply/badge appeared; code review of useChat.ts confirms updateMessage() is called per chunk inside the reader.read() loop, unchanged this cycle apart from the retry-loop wrapper around the same per-chunk logic.

criterion: The streaming assistant response renders progressively in the chat.
note: Bubble text grew incrementally (see FR-002-1) before settling on the final reply. Unaffected by this cycle.

criterion: No remaining purple color values exist in the shipped CSS/theme files.
note: theme.css untouched this cycle; dist CSS scan (grep of built index-*.css) confirmed the only accent hex present is #a0b9bf, with zero purple hex values.

criterion: Text and icon contrast against #A0B9BF surfaces remains readable, with no white-on-light-blue illegibility introduced.
note: User bubble text renders in dark (#16171b) on the #A0B9BF background; the new 'update' badge tone uses --color-accent-text (#3d5960, ~5.8:1 on --color-accent-soft per theme.css's own comment) rather than the near-black contrast color, deliberately chosen for readability on the lighter accent-soft badge background. Visibly legible in the screenshots.

criterion: The recolor is visual only - component behavior, layout, and functionality are unchanged.
note: Full send -> stream -> parse -> Supabase insert -> confirmation-badge flow (for <ADD>, <UPDATE>, malformed, retry, and unrecognized-title scenarios) completed correctly end-to-end this cycle, proving no functional regression from the theme (unchanged) or the new features.

criterion: An <UPDATE> produces a visually distinct 'rating updated' confirmation badge, distinguishable from the standard <ADD> 'logged' confirmation, added alongside the existing confirmation and the <RECOMMEND> card without disrupting layout or the #A0B9BF theme.
note: See FR-009-1.png: slate-blue-accent 'Rating updated · Inception' badge, visually distinct (different hue/border) from the green 'Saved · Inception' badge shown earlier in the same conversation thread, no layout disruption.

criterion: A deployed Netlify URL is provided that opens and works in a browser with no setup required by the client.
note: Deployment/repo-link/summary delivery happens in this pipeline's later docs/deploy step, not QA. This QA pass ran against a local production build (npm run build + local static server). Unaffected by this cycle's change_log.

criterion: The deployment is NOT a Lovable preview link.
note: Deployment/repo-link/summary delivery happens in this pipeline's later docs/deploy step, not QA. This QA pass ran against a local production build (npm run build + local static server). Unaffected by this cycle's change_log.

criterion: A GitHub repo link (or shared project) containing the full source is provided.
note: Deployment/repo-link/summary delivery happens in this pipeline's later docs/deploy step, not QA. This QA pass ran against a local production build (npm run build + local static server). Unaffected by this cycle's change_log.

criterion: A written summary of approximately half a page covering approach, key decisions, and tradeoffs is provided.
note: Deployment/repo-link/summary delivery happens in this pipeline's later docs/deploy step, not QA. This QA pass ran against a local production build (npm run build + local static server). Unaffected by this cycle's change_log.

criterion: (Route/app-loads smoke check only, per QA scope — FR-008 is untouched by this cycle's change_log entry.)
note: FR-008 (<RECOMMEND>) remains unimplemented in this codebase — confirmed by code review (createDefaultTagRegistry() registers only ADD/UPDATE; no RECOMMEND references anywhere in src/ or netlify/). This was first flagged as a gap in the Cycle-3 (v4) QA pass and this cycle's change_log explicitly scopes to FR-001/003/004/009 only, so per the touch-only-what's-required rule it was correctly left unbuilt rather than opportunistically added. The chat screen itself (which FR-008 would share) loads and functions correctly throughout this entire run — demonstrated by every FR-001/002/003/004/005/009 scenario above, none of which crashed or regressed.
